title: Ex-Offender Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Ex-Offender Counseling 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST BEAVERTON MULTICULTURAL COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following services: mental health counseling for individuals, families, and groups, with a focus on trauma, life transitions, grief, PTSD, depression, and anxiety. Support groups and community wellness groups. Mental health evaluations, including assessment, diagnosis, and treatment. Substance use disorder (SUD) treatment: screening, assessment, individual and group counseling, DUII diversion, and conviction support. Peer support and case management to help navigate resources. Psychiatric services, including medication management, for current clients. In-person and telehealth options are available. 3800 SW Cedar Hills Boulevard Suite 288 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Low-income households with a focus on those who have CareOregon (OHP) Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 731-9589 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST PORTLAND OFFICE MULTICULTURAL COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following services: mental health counseling for individuals, families, and groups, with a focus on trauma, life transitions, grief, PTSD, depression, and anxiety. Support groups and community wellness groups. Mental health evaluations, including assessment, diagnosis, and treatment. Substance use disorder (SUD) treatment: screening, assessment, individual and group counseling, DUII diversion, and conviction support. Peer support and case management to help navigate resources. Psychiatric services, including medication management, for current clients. In-person and telehealth options are available. 7931 NE Halsey Street Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Low-income households with a focus on those who have CareOregon (OHP) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-2:30pm. After-hours counseling appointments available. Email (503) 731-9589 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION REENTRY SERVICES EX-OFFENDER RESOURCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A resource center supporting individuals transitioning from incarceration, those on probation or parole, and others affected by the criminal justice system. Offers weekly legal clinics for civil matters, help with health insurance applications, and classes on parenting, personal finance, tenant readiness, and motivation. Provides consultations, information, and referrals for people and families impacted by incarceration. A computer lab is available for tutoring, GED preparation, job searches, and other needs. 2085 Commercial Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or past involvement with criminal justice system. Preference given to those currently on parole supervision and recently released from incarceration. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 332-5332 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST MULTICULTURAL COUNSELING SERVICES LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST MULTICULTURAL COUNSELING SERVICES MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY EX-OFFENDER RESOURCE PROGRAM
